When it comes to business compliance and taxation in Ethiopia, inside experience is everything. ⚖️💼

We are thrilled to introduce Wendimu Addise as our guest for Episode 4 of Insights Unplugged! With over 14 years of experience as a Legal Expert at the Ministry of Revenues (የገቢዎች ሚኒስቴር), Wendimu brings unmatched, practical knowledge on the regulatory frameworks every founder MUST understand.

He has seen exactly why some startups thrive and why others are shut down due to simple legal misunderstandings. If you want to navigate the Ethiopian tax code with confidence, this is the expert you need to hear from.

#InsightsUnplugged #OrbitInnovationHub #LegalExpert #MinistryOfRevenues #EthiopianStartups #BusinessCompliance #FounderAdvice

Think a small delay in paying your business taxes won't hurt? Think again. 🚨

In this exclusive trailer for Episode 4 of Insights Unplugged, legal expert Wendimu Addise breaks down a practical scenario every Ethiopian entrepreneur needs to understand. He explains exactly how compounding penalties, late fees, and interest can turn a minor tax obligation into a business-ending crisis.

This is true Taxation 101 for Founders. If you aren't paying attention to your compliance deadlines, you are putting your entire company at risk. 

We cover all the "ድርጅትዎን የሚያዘጉ የህግ ስህተቶች!" in this crucial episode.

#InsightsUnplugged #OrbitInnovationHub #BusinessCompliance #EthiopianStartups #FounderAdvice #LegalTips #Taxation #Entrepreneurship

Your brilliant product won't save your startup if the government shuts you down. 🚨

Too many founders treat legal compliance, business licenses, and taxes as an "afterthought"—something to figure out after the business makes money. 

But as legal expert Wendimu Addise explains in Episode 4 of Insights Unplugged, ignorance of the law is the fastest way to kill your company.

If you are an Ethiopian entrepreneur, you cannot afford to skip this episode. 

We dive deep into:
⚖️ Taxation 101 for Founders: Understanding your obligations before the penalties hit.

Stop crossing your fingers and hoping for the best. Build your business on a solid legal foundation.

The product you build today is not what defines your tomorrow. It is the identity and character you build within yourself today that makes you a great leader."

In the fast-paced startup ecosystem, it is easy to get consumed by product development, pitch decks, and funding rounds. But as business and investment expert Solomon Sisay shares in our latest episode, true sustainable success starts with self-leadership.

When we talk about leadership, we have to look at two distinct halves:
1️⃣ The Person: Your character, credibility, and personal discipline.
2️⃣ The Process: The operational systems and execution structures you build.
Without personal discipline, even the most brilliant, highly-funded startup ideas are like a flashy, modern car with no engine—it looks impressive on paper, but it is not going anywhere.

Solomon Sisay also shares a vital challenge for African founders: we must build a generation of business leaders who read, continuously educate themselves, and understand global economic realities.

Are you actively building your leadership character alongside your product? Let's discuss in the comments. 👇

📺 Catch the full masterclass on Episode 3 of Insights Unplugged on our YouTube channel: https://lnkd.in/eNgrKFDm

#InsightsUnplugged #LeadershipInBusiness #OrbitInnovationHub #EthiopianStartups #ExecutiveDiscipline #Scale #SelfLeadership #AfricaBusiness

Program Coordinator Role

Are you passionate about supporting MSMEs, startups, and entrepreneurs to grow through digital platforms and market access opportunities? 

Click here to apply: https://lnkd.in/eajattc6 

Orbit Innovation Hub is looking for a proactive and collaborative Program Coordinator to support the implementation of Digital Market Linkage activities under the SEED Program funded by the Mastercard Foundation. 

The role focuses on strengthening the digital presence, market access, and growth opportunities of MSMEs and startups across Ethiopia.

Key areas of engagement include:
• Supporting MSMEs and startups in accessing digital markets and e-commerce opportunities
• Coordinating ecosystem partnerships and stakeholder engagement
• Organizing outreach programs, events, and digital market linkage activities
• Supporting entrepreneurs in strengthening their online presence and visibility

Requirements:
✔️ Bachelor’s degree in Project Management, Business Administration, Marketing, Value Chain Management, or related fields
✔️ Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience
✔️ Experience working within the MSME/startup ecosystem
✔️ Strong communication, coordination, and relationship-building skills
✔️ Passion for entrepreneurship, digital innovation, and social impact
📍 Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (with frequent regional travel)

👩‍💼 Female candidates are highly encouraged to apply.

Join us in building stronger digital opportunities for Ethiopian MSMEs and entrepreneurs!

Safeguarding Commitment
Orbit Innovation Hub is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of all individuals engaged in our programs. All staff are expected to uphold this commitment by adhering to safeguarding policies and maintaining a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment.

OIH adopts a zero-tolerance approach to any form of abuse, exploitation, harassment, corruption, or unethical conduct.

🚀 Recapping the Digital Market Linkage Lab Initiative in Jimma

Rolled out the SEED Program's onboarding and launch activities in Jimma. ✅

The initiative brought together Medium Enterprises, Interns, and Digital Marketing Experts for a collaborative 7‑week journey focused on strengthening digital presence, improving market linkage, and job creation for MSMEs and local businesses. 🤝

In partnership with Scopus Research, Training and consultancy plc, Debo Engineering & DECx, participants engaged in insightful discussions, networking, and practical onboarding sessions designed to bridge gaps between businesses and digital transformation opportunities. 💡

Beyond enterprise growth, the initiative is also creating valuable learning opportunities for young professionals through hands‑on internship experiences in digital marketing and business support. 🎓

This recap video captures some of the energy, collaboration, and shared vision from the onboarding sessions — and this is just the beginning. 🎥 We will also be sharing interview videos featuring participating enterprises, interns, and experts in the coming days. 📢

161 enterprises • 3 cities • 1 shared vision for growth. 🌍

🚀 Innovation in action in Debre Birhan.

Orbit Innovation Hub had the privilege of attending a significant milestone event as Millennium Girma -CEO-Rumi Technology Solutions, a team of young Ethiopian innovators, officially handed over digital systems developed for the North Shewa Education Bureau and the North Shewa Civil Service Bureau.

This moment is incredibly special for us: Millennium Girma -CEO-Rumi Technology Solutions one of Orbit Innovation Hub's brilliant incubees during our third cohort a couple of years back.

Watching their growth from an early-stage startup in our program to a team delivering major public sector solutions fills us with immense pride.

These technology solutions are designed to support digital transformation in institutional systems, particularly in the education sector by streamlining school administration and improving operational efficiency through technology.

The event also marked an important signing and technology transfer, highlighting how young innovators are building practical solutions that public institutions can adopt and trust.

What makes this milestone even more inspiring is that the team behind Rumi is still pursuing their education while simultaneously building technology with real-world impact.

At Orbit Innovation Hub, we are proud to witness innovation move beyond ideas and into institutions — creating solutions that improve systems, empower communities, and inspire the next generation of entrepreneurs.

🌟 This is what young innovation in action looks like.

You have 0 revenue, no product, but your business card says "CEO". 
Sounds familiar? 🚩

In the early days of a startup, founders spend way too much time arguing over C-level titles and not enough time on execution. In Episode 2 of Insights Unplugged, Abel Tefera Balkew and Dawit Teshome Gebresenbet break down why you need to strip away the startup ego and focus on building a team that actually delivers.

Catch the full episode to learn how to assign real roles, handle founder conflicts, and build a lasting culture.

🔗 Link in the comments! 👇

Is starting a business with your best friend a recipe for success—or a guaranteed disaster? 🤔

It’s tempting to co-found a startup with your inner circle because the trust is already there. But sharing the same hobbies doesn't mean you share the same work ethic.

In Episode 2 of Insights Unplugged, host Dawit Teshome Gebresenbet sits down with team formation expert Abel Tefera Balkew to discuss the messy, complicated reality of building a startup team in the Ethiopian ecosystem.

We dive into:
🚫 The "Clone Trap" – Why hiring people exactly like you limits growth.
🤝 Friends vs. Founders – How to separate personal relationships from business execution.
⚖️ Defining Clear Roles – Why "we are all equal co-founders" is a terrible strategy.

If you are a solo founder looking for a partner or a team struggling to make decisions, this is your playbook.

🎓✨ In the city of wonder, where vision has stood strong for generations, a new story of builders, dreamers, and entrepreneurs is beginning.

Orbit Innovation Hub proudly celebrated the graduation of Cohort 7 in Lalibela — not just as a ceremony, but as a moment that reflected growth, ambition, and possibility. 🚀

Opening the program, Orbit Innovation Hub Manager, Dawit Teshome Gebresenbet, congratulated the graduates and expressed deep appreciation to all stakeholders and partners who supported the journey. He emphasized that this milestone is only the beginning, highlighting Orbit’s continued commitment to launching new initiatives and creating opportunities that empower young innovators to lead change.

During his remarks, Lalibela Mayor Mesay Wedaje shared that he had also attended the launching program and was amazed by how far the cohort had come. He described the graduates as “like gold in the making” — small in number, but rich in potential, value, and impact.

He further encouraged them to take responsibility as pioneers, creating opportunities and jobs for others through innovation and entrepreneurship. Calling it “the beginning, not the end,” he also extended his appreciation to Orbit Innovation Hub for initiating and driving such an impactful program in Lalibela.

From a place where history is carved in stone to a generation carving their future through ideas — the journey continues. 🇪🇹✨

Promoting Inclusive Market linkage  for Enterprises. 🤝💡

A successful Capacity Building Training on Digital Market Linkage (DML) was conducted for Persons with Disabilities (PWDs) in Burayu in collaboration with Burayu Polytechnic College.

The training focused on strengthening participants’ understanding of digital market linkage and building pathways toward greater economic participation and collaboration. As part of the broader DML Lab initiative, participants will also engage in opportunities focused on access to markets, digital financing, and job creation, while fostering collective growth and collaboration.

This initiative reflects a continued commitment to inclusion, empowerment, and sustainable livelihoods through digital innovation and skills development.

About Orbit Innovation Hub
Orbit Innovation Hub (OIH), an initiative of eHealth IT Services PLC, is a dynamic organization dedicated to supporting startups and MSMEs through incubation, training, and capacity building.

✨ Position Overview
We are seeking a passionate and results-driven Program Coordinator to lead our Startup Incubation Program. This role is central to attracting high-potential startups, supporting their growth, and strengthening the entrepreneurial ecosystem.

🔑 Key Responsibilities
• Lead and manage incubation program operations
• Design strategies to attract and select promising startups
• Provide coaching, workshops, and mentorship support
• Build partnerships and represent the program at events
• Prepare reports and support fundraising efforts
• Ensure safeguarding principles are integrated into all program activities, maintaining a safe, inclusive, and respectful environment for all participants

🎓 Qualifications & Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in business, Management, Marketing, or related field
• Experience in project management and working with startups/MSMEs
• Strong communication, organizational, and analytical skills
• Fluency in English & Amharic (additional languages are a plus)

🌟 What We’re Looking For
A proactive team player with strong leadership skills and a passion for entrepreneurship and innovation
• Strong commitment to safeguarding, inclusion, and ethical conduct

🛡 Safeguarding Commitment
Orbit Innovation Hub is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of all individuals engaged in our programs. All staff are expected to uphold this commitment by adhering to safeguarding policies and maintaining a safe and respectful environment.
“Orbit adopts a zero-tolerance approach to any form of abuse, exploitation, harassment, corruption, or unethical conduct”

The secret to scaling isn't just a good idea.

Many founders believe a great product is enough, but the reality is that strong ideas grow faster with the right support system. Without a network to stress-test your assumptions, growth can stagnate.

To move from "idea" to "impact," focus on these three pillars:
1️⃣ Seek Mentorship: Learn from those who have already navigated the pitfalls.
2️⃣ Market Validation: Don’t build in a vacuum. Talk to your users early and often.
3️⃣ Consistent Execution: Small, daily progress beats occasional bursts of brilliance.

At Orbit Innovation Hub, we provide the ecosystem to make this happen.

What’s the best piece of advice you’ve received as a founder? Share it in the comments! 👇

🚀 Reflecting on the Launch of the Digital Market Linkage Lab Initiative in #Adama for #MeduimEnterprises

We recently marked an exciting milestone with the successful launch of the Digital Market Linkage Lab Initiative in Adama under the #SEEDProgram.

The event brought together medium enterprises, digital marketing experts, and ecosystem partners, all united by a shared vision—strengthening businesses through digital transformation. A total of 45 Medium enterprises embarked on a 7-week journey, working closely with Kassma Digitals and ENKOY Technologies consultants to turn identified gaps into practical, measurable improvements in their digital presence and market reach.

What made this initiative particularly impactful was its dual purpose.

Beyond supporting business growth, it also created meaningful opportunities for young professionals. Through structured internships, emerging talent gained hands-on experience in digital marketing while directly contributing to the success of local enterprises. The energy and collaboration between businesses and youth participants were truly inspiring.

The launch event itself was filled with optimism, engagement, and a strong sense of purpose. It set the tone for what would become a collaborative effort to connect enterprises with market information, digital finance opportunities, and new growth pathways.

Looking back, the initiative was more than just a program—it was a step toward building a more inclusive, digitally empowered business ecosystem in Adama.

Strengthening Jimma’s MSMEs to Lead in the Digital Economy

MSMEs are the backbone of Ethiopia’s economy. To drive meaningful transformation, we must ensure they are equipped to compete in an increasingly digital world.

Last week, Orbit Innovation Hub, under the #SEEDProgram, was on the ground in Jimma. In collaboration with the Oromia JCV Bureau BCHUOO, we hosted intensive, hands-on capacity-building sessions at Jimma TVET College.

Our mission is to bridge the digital divide by empowering MSMEs with the skills to thrive online. Through this Digital Market Linkage capacity-building initiative, MSMEs are learning how to:

📱 Leverage digital platforms to strategically expand their customer base.

🛍 Showcase their products and services to connect with broader markets.

💡 Build a credible, sustainable digital presence that drives growth.

🤝 This is more than just a session; it marks a significant milestone in our commitment to building a resilient, digitally-enabled MSME ecosystem in Ethiopia. We are proud to be part of their journey from survival to digital leadership.

Together, we can create a future where our MSMEs not only survive but thrive in the digital economy!

Community Spotlight | 7 Lakes Entrepreneurial Community – Bishoftu 🌊✨

In Bishoftu, the Cohort 6 Graduation was more than a celebration of startup milestones — it was also a defining moment for the 7 Lakes Entrepreneurial Community.

While the incubation program focused on strengthening individual startups, Orbit Innovation Hub simultaneously invested in something equally important: building a strong, self-driven local entrepreneurial network. Bishoftu was one of the cities where this vision began to take shape in a powerful way. 🚀

Through a careful selection and assessment process, local leaders were identified and engaged to champion entrepreneurship within their city. 

These leaders participated in a structured capacity-building program in Addis Ababa, where they refined their leadership approach, strengthened their ecosystem understanding, and co-created a practical roadmap tailored to Bishoftu’s context. 🧠📍

From this foundation, the 7 Lakes Community was formed.
In a short period of time, the community has:
Mobilized a committed and growing network of entrepreneurs
Created regular spaces for connection and peer learning
Initiated collaborative activities that encourage shared growth
Begun positioning itself as a central platform for entrepreneurship in Bishoftu

During the graduation event, the 7 Lakes team took the stage not just to report progress, but to share a vision. They outlined their journey so far, presented their action plan, invited stakeholder input, and opened the floor for partnership opportunities. 🤝

The session sparked meaningful engagement and demonstrated the city’s readiness to nurture its own entrepreneurial ecosystem. What stood out most was the sense of ownership — this is no longer an externally driven initiative, but a locally anchored movement.

The 7 Lakes Community is steadily becoming a platform where entrepreneurs in Bishoftu connect, collaborate, and collectively shape the future of innovation in their city. 🌍💡

#OrbitInnovationHub #7LakesCommunity #Bishoftu #EcosystemBuilding #EntrepreneurshipEthiopia #LocalLeadership #Cohort6 #InnovationHub #CommunityDevelopment #EthiopiaTech

Community Highlight | Fasiledes Entrepreneurial Community

The Gondar Fasilides Entrepreneurs Community Highlight was one of the key events held alongside the Cohort 6 Incubation Program Graduation Ceremony in Gondar.

Beyond launching the incubation program, Orbit Innovation Hub also introduced a Community Development Track aimed at strengthening local entrepreneurial leadership and ecosystem ownership. 🚀💡

As part of this initiative, community leaders were identified and assessed across all Cohort 6 incubation cities. 📍 Selected leaders were then brought to Addis Ababa for a two-day intensive capacity-building training. Following the training, each city team developed a six-month action plan focused on activating their local ecosystem and fostering sustainable community leadership. 🧠📋

In Gondar, this effort led to the establishment of the Gondar Fasilides Entrepreneurs Community Team. Within just two months, the team has:

Built a core network of 35+ active members

Organized networking coffee meetups to strengthen collaboration

Hosted launching events

Conducted various capacity-building trainings for members

During the Community Highlight session at the graduation event, startups, community leaders, and key stakeholders came together to mark the transition from planning to implementation. 

The team presented their progress, gathered feedback on next steps, and made a call for partnership and sponsorship.

As a result, they secured:
A free one-year training package from a consulting firm
A dedicated meeting space provided by the city polytechnic college

The event clearly demonstrated what is possible when local leaders are empowered, action plans are owned, and communities take the lead in shaping their entrepreneurial future. 🌍💪✨

🔵 Strengthening Government–Startup Engagement in Wolkite 🤝

As part of the Cohort 6 SEED Incubation Program graduation in Wolkite, a panel discussion brought together public institutions and startup founders to explore how government actors can better support early-stage ventures as they grow beyond incubation.

• Murad Yassin, Gurage Zone Job Creation Head Office
• Tariku Dagye, Wolkite City Administration Justice Head Office

The session was moderated by Fasil Getaneh, Program Coordinator at Orbit Innovation Hub, SEED Incubation Program.

The conversation focused on practical areas where startups and public institutions intersect, including:
• The importance of clear co-founder agreements to prevent disputes and protect businesses as they grow
• Access to capacity building, BDS training, and advisory services through government initiatives
• Support around market linkage, legal awareness, and finance-related guidance
• The role of public institutions in creating an enabling environment for entrepreneurship at the local level

Panelists emphasized that startups benefit most when they proactively engage public institutions as partners, while institutions play a key role in supporting sustainable enterprise development through coordinated services and guidance.

The discussion reinforced a shared understanding: a strong startup ecosystem is built through dialogue, clarity, and collaboration between founders and the institutions that serve them.

📢 Call for Consulting Firms – Digital Market Linkage (DML) Gap Assessment

Orbit Innovation Hub (OIH), under the SEED Program, invites qualified consulting firms to undertake a field-based Digital Market Linkage (DML) Lab gap assessment for Medium Enterprises (MEs).

This assignment aims to support the scale-up and effective rollout of the DML Lab initiative by outsourcing the implementation of an existing OIH-developed gap assessment tool, while incorporating an independent, third-party perspective.

The selected consulting firm will conduct assessments, develop enterprise-level business profiles, and produce an overall analytical overview to inform the continued design and targeting of the DML Lab.

🔍 Scope of the Assignment Includes:

1. Field-based DML gap assessment using OIH’s standardized tool

2. Assessment, filtering, and shortlisting of Medium Enterprises

3. Development of enterprise-level business profile reports

4. Data analysis and preparation of an overall analytical overview report

📍 Geographic Coverage:

Initial cities: Wolaita Sodo, Adama, and Bishoftu

Potential expansion: Additional cities in Amhara, Tigray, Oromia, and the Southern cluster/region (subject to program needs)

📊 Scale of Assessment:

Minimum: 135 Medium Enterprises (across the three initial cities)

Maximum: Up to 410 Medium Enterprises (including potential expansion cities)

⏱️ Duration:

20 calendar days, inclusive of fieldwork, analysis, and reporting

👥 Who Should Apply:

1. Legally registered consulting firms with valid VAT registration

2. Firms with regional staff or field presence

3. Demonstrated experience in enterprise diagnostics, market studies, digital transformation, or digital market linkage initiatives

🔴 Event Recap: Unlocking Finance & Market Opportunities for Entrepreneurs | GEW 2025 🚀🌍

Orbit Innovation Hub successfully hosted a transformative session on “Connecting the Dots: Finance & Digital Market Linkage Across Sectors”, bringing together Ethiopia’s key entrepreneurship players to explore practical pathways for MSME growth. 🇪🇹✨

Key Highlights:


💬 Panel Insights:

Alazar Negesu (Kifiya Financial Technology PLC) shared how digital platforms and fintech solutions are expanding MSME access to finance, addressing challenges like limited credit history and onboarding barriers.

Tamerat Zewdie (Arada Mart) highlighted the power of e-commerce platforms in connecting local products to wider markets and scaling opportunities for MSMEs.

Yona Y (Entrepreneurship Development Institute (EDI) - Ethiopia) emphasized on-the-ground lessons from incubation and SEED programs, pointing to critical skill gaps and the need for cross-sector collaboration to strengthen Ethiopia’s entrepreneurial ecosystem.

🐆 Cheetah Generation Stories:

SEMERE KIDU GEBREKIDAN (African Market Group): Post-war challenges could have stalled progress, but Orbit Innovation Hub’s incubation, Digital Market Linkage (DML), and community development support enabled him to rebuild trade and market access in Tigray. Semere highlighted the need for startup support, device financing, and continued community development programs to empower entrepreneurs in fragile contexts.

Rediet Alemayehu of the Rediet SHeMan Design: From a single sewing machine to an international brand exporting to Europe and the U.S., Rediet showcased how mindset, grit, and ecosystem support: including a women-oriented cohort, can propel Ethiopian manufacturing globally. She stressed sustainable collaboration and ongoing ecosystem backing for startups.

Eskender Bekele, "Hope"pressurized irrigation system company HOPE: With innovative irrigation solutions addressing water scarcity, Eskinder shared how Orbit supported product development, finance, and workspace access, helping him scale impact and win global recognition.

📌 Final Remarks by Yonathan Yilma Yona Y(Entrepreneurship Development Institute (EDI) - Ethiopia ):
 Collaboration is key, no organization can do it alone. Entrepreneurs must leverage their full capacities, while financial institutions and ecosystem partners have smooth access to finance, mentorship, and market linkages to accelerate MSME growth.

✨ Impact in Action:
Through startup showcases, panel discussions, and evidence-based insights, today’s session highlighted real-world solutions to strengthen Ethiopia’s innovation ecosystem, paving the way for youth- and women-led enterprises to thrive.

Unleashing the Cheetah Generation — One Founder at a Time 🐆

The “Cheetah Generation” is more than a metaphor — it’s a mindset, and Rediet SHeMan Design embodies it fully.

While many waited for perfect conditions, Rediet started with a single sewing machine in her living room and built SheMan Design into a brand exporting to Europe and the U.S. No shortcuts. No waiting rooms. Just speed, grit, and a bold belief that Ethiopian manufacturing can compete globally.
Tomorrow, she joins our founder-led conversation to share:

Unleashing the Cheetah Generation — Rebuilding Markets From the Ground Up 🐆

🌍Every cheetah story begins with someone who refuses to slow down.

For SEMERE KIDU GEBREKIDAN, Founder of African Market Group, that moment came when he saw communities in Tigray cut off from trade, supply chains, and basic market access.

Instead of waiting for traditional systems to recover, Semere built a digital pathway of his own. African Market Group is now creating a tech-enabled marketplace that reconnects farmers, traders, and consumers — proving that innovation can thrive even in the most fragile environments.

This is what the Cheetah Generation looks like:
 Not speed for the sake of speed, but speed with purpose — rebuilding trust, restoring livelihoods, and showing that technology can become the backbone of recovery.

On Tuesday, Semere will dive into:
 🔹 How African Market Group is redesigning commerce in post-conflict settings
 🔹 The practical realities of rebuilding a market ecosystem from zero
 🔹 What Ethiopia needs to do to truly Unleash the Cheetah Generation in all regions

A powerful story of resilience, technology, and determination.

🚀 Enabling Arba Minch Enterprises!

Last week, our Digital Market Linkage (DML) Lab under the SEED Program engaged 40 local enterprises, 30 led by men and 10 by women across sectors from manufacturing to agriculture, together employing 755 hardworking individuals (253 women).

Here’s what we learned 

✨ Key Insights
📌 Most businesses still rely on word-of-mouth and traditional marketing.
📌 Social media is used but not strategically enough to drive sales.
📌 Access to finance and skilled labor remains a major barrier to growth.

💡 What’s Next?
We’re on a mission to boost visibility, digital skills, and market access, unlocking job creation and sustainable growth for the Arba Minch business community. 🚀

👥 Snapshot:

Total employees: 755 (502 men | 253 women)

💻Digital readiness is low overall, even though most businesses are confident in their capabilities.

⚙️Top challenges: Unskilled manpower, low digital literacy, limited time, low awareness

💰Finance needs: Working capital, equipment, and expansion, hindered by collateral & bureaucracy

🌾Job creation potential: many enterprises are planning expansion, particularly in manufacturing and agriculture sectors.

🚀 Empowering Ecosystem Leaders Across Ethiopia

As we wrap up the Entrepreneurship Community Leaders Capacity Building Training, we celebrate a powerful journey of learning, collaboration, and ecosystem activation. Leaders from Axum, Mekelle, Gonder, Kombolcha, Wolkite, Hawassa, and Bishoftu came together with a shared mission — to ignite entrepreneurship and strengthen community-led innovation across Ethiopia.

This training focused on equipping ecosystem champions with practical skills in:
✅ Ecosystem thinking & collaboration
✅ Entrepreneurial mindset & innovation
✅ Community leadership & engagement

🌍 SEED’s Journey of Impact

In just two years, Orbit Innovation Hub, through the SEED Program, has incubated 377 startups across 12+ cities, with 300 of them accessing seed grant facilitation. We are proud to have supported startups that have already created 1,000+ jobs! 🚀

But we’re just getting started! Over the next three years, we will incubate the remaining 623 startups, reaching our 5-year target of 1,000 innovative businesses outside of Addis Ababa through mentorship, training, and incubation.

Saturday’s inauguration perfectly captured this journey of growth and impact. Our Co-Founder & COO, Girum Assefa Habtewold, highlighted the incredible success of Cohort 5, our first women-lead cohort. From 3 cities, 75 startups graduated, with 95% being women-led—a true reflection of our commitment to inclusive growth. ✨

His words to the founders remain a powerful reminder for every entrepreneur: "Stay strong and remain committed. At the end of the tunnel, there is always light; and with persistence, things will change for the better."

A Monumental Day for Ethiopian Entrepreneurship: 
Recap of SEED Cohort 5 Inauguration & Community Development Day!

What an absolutely inspiring and impactful day we experienced at the Sheger City SEED Cohort Five Inauguration & Community Development Day at Skylight Hotel! It was a powerful gathering celebrating innovation, inclusive growth, and the immense potential of Ethiopian entrepreneurs.

Key Highlights from the Day:
🎤 Vision & Commitment: Girum Assefa Habtewold (COO, Orbit Innovation Hub) lauded our women-only Cohort 5, proudly noting that 95% of the 75 graduating startups are women-owned. His message of perseverance resonated deeply: "Stay strong and remain committed. At the end of the tunnel, there is always light!"

Dr. Hassan Hussein (CEO, Entrepreneurship Development Institute (EDI) - Ethiopia) shared exciting news about the new Entrepreneurship Policy and upcoming Startup Law, emphasizing that "this is the right time for startups to grow." He underscored success factors: "market, money, and management," and the wisdom to "never let it go no matter the cost."

🚀 Innovation in Action: 
Startup Pitches Jalene C. (DNA TECHS) showcased data analysis democratization for all businesses. Aminat Mohamed (DEKUS TOMATO POWDER) presented a sustainable food solution for healthier communities. Rediet SHeMan Design (SHeMan Design & Ethio Fabrics) highlighted eco-friendly fashion rooted in Ethiopian culture.

🔬 Thriving Exhibition Floor: 
The event truly came alive with all 75 Cohort 5 startups showcasing their innovative solutions! We saw game-changing ideas across Agri-Tech, Health & Wellness, Tech Platforms, and Sustainable Manufacturing. The diversity and ingenuity were incredible!

📜 Celebrating the Achievers: 
Graduation! The proudest moment was witnessing all 75 startups receive their Certificates of Completion! This marks their transition to market-ready businesses, ready to make a significant impact.

👋 The Journey Ahead: Closing Remarks Dawit Teshome Gebresenbet (Program Lead) eloquently concluded: "This Certificate is your license to continue learning, continue innovating, and continue leading. The ecosystem is counting on your success, and Orbit Innovation Hub will remain your supportive partner on this journey.”
A heartfelt thank you to all our partners, mentors, investors, and every community member who contributed to this monumental success. The future of Ethiopian entrepreneurship is undeniably bright, female-led, and primed for global impact!
